	•	It gently moves the lead from a label (“left hip tight”) to a timeline
	•	It helps them start reflecting, not just answering

This is classic NEPQ problem-awareness, not interrogation.

⸻

3.⁠ ⁠“…and how has that been showing up for you on the course?” — impact without pressure

This is the most important part of the sentence.

We deliberately say “showing up” instead of:
	•	“causing”
	•	“affecting badly”
	•	“holding you back”

Why?
	•	“Showing up” is neutral and non-judgmental
	•	It invites observation, not defense
	•	It lets them connect the dots between body → performance

We’re guiding them to self-discover:

“Oh yeah… this actually does affect my swing / consistency / comfort.”

That’s far more powerful than us telling them.
